chapter_title.ar: باب الْبُكَاءِ وَ الدُّعَاءِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – The weeping and the supplication during the Salāt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/80)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/80)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ ذِكْرِ السُّورَةِ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ يَدْعُو بِهَا فِي الصَّلَاةِ مِثْلَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ فَقَالَ إِذَا كُنْتَ تَدْعُو بِهَا فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Ubeyd Bin Zurara who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about mentioning the Chapter from the Book to supplicate with during the Salāt, like [112:1] Say: He Allah is One (Chapter 112)’. So heasws said: ‘When you were supplicating by it, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurarah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about reading a chapter of the book in the form of an appeal for help in Salah (prayer) like Chapter 112. He (the Imam) said, ‘If you read it in the form of appeal for help it is not harmful.’”
